name: getMcpTools
description: Call this whenever a user request cannot be fulfilled by your local tools alone, or when you are unsure whether a suitable local tool exists. Lists all tools exposed by configured remote MCP servers. Returned tool names are namespaced as mcp.<namespace>.<tool>, for example mcp.jenny-mcp.getTime. Use the optional query parameter with a short single keyword (e.g. "confluence", "time", "jira") to filter results — do not pass full sentences or phrases as query. After this call you MUST call the configured MCP execution tool (default: getMcp) to execute the matching tool. Keep this manifest at version 1.
policyHint: getMcpTools is a discovery step only — it is never the final tool call. After getMcpTools returns a tool list, you MUST immediately call the configured MCP execution tool (default: getMcp) with the matching mcp.<namespace>.<tool> name to execute the user request. Never answer the user after getMcpTools without also calling that execution tool first. Skipping the execution step after getMcpTools is not allowed. Tool names starting with mcp. are NEVER valid local tool calls — they must always be routed through the configured MCP execution tool.

server.description: Optional configured MCP server name. If omitted, lists tools for all configured servers.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive filter applied to remote tool names and descriptions. Use this for task-focused discovery, e.g. "time" for current time/date requests.
